name in kana (P1814) == * のきじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1280005002351 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県安来市能義町366', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:能義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11612065}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Noki Shrine |画像 = [[File:Noki Shrine.jpg|270px]]
Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}{{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114}} Nogi-chō 366 |位置 = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-32|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}} |別名 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = Shinkō-sai |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Noki Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Noki Shrine''' (のきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Nogi-chō, {{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === Current deities are as follows. *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} In the entry for Noki station in Ou District of the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858}}'', Noki Ōkami appears. The original deity is considered to have been this Noki Ōkami, and there is a theory that Noki Ōkami and Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto are the same deity''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.40.. === History === It is identified with “Noki-sha,” a Shrine Bureau shrine in the Ou District section of ''Izumo no Kuni Fudoki,'' and with “Noki Shrine” in Ou District, Izumo Province, in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}''; but there is also a theory identifying it with “Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine” in Nogi District in the same text. Between this shrine and Kunitsu Daimyōjin in Kisa village (now {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shibusa Shrine|UNKNOWN}}), there were disputes over candidate status twice: in the {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku}} era and early {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.42.. According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'', the {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} granted to Nogi deity was Junior Fifth Upper in {{ill|lt=867|Q23899|WD=Q23899}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635}} 9) and Senior Fifth Lower in {{ill|lt=871|Q23903|WD=Q23903}} (Jōgan 13). However, it is unclear whether these were granted to Noki Shrine or to Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine. When shrine buildings were lost in a fire in {{ill|lt=1611|Q6768|WD=Q6768}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 16), old records were also lost. In early modern times it was revered as the sōsha of Nogi District. It was ranked gōsha in {{ill|lt=Meiji 4|Q11512280|WD=Q11512280}}, then promoted to prefectural shrine in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}). === Ancient historic sites === In the precinct is the Noki Shrine site, considered to be dwelling remains from the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858}} to {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}}. Nearby are several {{ill|lt=round tumuli|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747}}, including Noki Shrine Okuin Kofun. === Festivals === *February - {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710}} *June 5 - {{ill|lt=Otaue Festival||WD=|lt=Rice-planting festival}} *June 30 - {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910}} *July 31 - {{ill|lt=Summer festival|Q11430090|WD=Q11430090}} *October 19 - {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}} *November 23 - {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518}} === Notes === === References === *{{ill|lt=Takine Yoshiyuki|Q17160660|WD=Q17160660}} ''Dictionary of Ancient Izumo'', 2001, {{ill|lt=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|WD=Q11501312}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Tanigawa Kenichi|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}, ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San’in'' (new edition), 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}} ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Noki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Noki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧県社]] [[Category:安来市の建築物]] [[Category:安来市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * のきじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1280005002351 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県安来市能義町366', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:能義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11612065}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Noki Shrine |画像 = [[File:Noki Shrine.jpg|270px]]
Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}{{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114}} Nogi-chō 366 |位置 = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-32|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}} |別名 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = Shinkō-sai |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Noki Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Noki Shrine''' (のきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Nogi-chō, {{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === Current deities are as follows. *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} In the entry for Noki station in Ou District of the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858}}'', Noki Ōkami appears. The original deity is considered to have been this Noki Ōkami, and there is a theory that Noki Ōkami and Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto are the same deity''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.40.. === History === It is identified with “Noki-sha,” a Shrine Bureau shrine in the Ou District section of ''Izumo no Kuni Fudoki,'' and with “Noki Shrine” in Ou District, Izumo Province, in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}''; but there is also a theory identifying it with “Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine” in Nogi District in the same text. Between this shrine and Kunitsu Daimyōjin in Kisa village (now {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shibusa Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}), there were disputes over candidate status twice: in the {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku}} era and early {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.42.. According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'', the {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} granted to Nogi deity was Junior Fifth Upper in {{ill|lt=867|Q23899|WD=Q23899}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635}} 9) and Senior Fifth Lower in {{ill|lt=871|Q23903|WD=Q23903}} (Jōgan 13). However, it is unclear whether these were granted to Noki Shrine or to Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine. When shrine buildings were lost in a fire in {{ill|lt=1611|Q6768|WD=Q6768}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 16), old records were also lost. In early modern times it was revered as the sōsha of Nogi District. It was ranked gōsha in {{ill|lt=Meiji 4|Q11512280|WD=Q11512280}}, then promoted to prefectural shrine in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}). === Ancient historic sites === In the precinct is the Noki Shrine site, considered to be dwelling remains from the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858}} to {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}}. Nearby are several {{ill|lt=round tumuli|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747}}, including Noki Shrine Okuin Kofun. === Festivals === *February - {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710}} *June 5 - {{ill|lt=Otaue Festival||WD=|lt=Rice-planting festival}} *June 30 - {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910}} *July 31 - {{ill|lt=Summer festival|Q11430090|WD=Q11430090}} *October 19 - {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}} *November 23 - {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518}} === Notes === === References === *{{ill|lt=Takine Yoshiyuki|Q17160660|WD=Q17160660}} ''Dictionary of Ancient Izumo'', 2001, {{ill|lt=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|WD=Q11501312}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Tanigawa Kenichi|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}, ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San’in'' (new edition), 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}} ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Noki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Noki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧県社]] [[Category:安来市の建築物]] [[Category:安来市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * のきじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1280005002351 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県安来市能義町366', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:能義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11612065}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Noki Shrine |画像 = [[File:Noki Shrine.jpg|270px]]
Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}{{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114}} Nogi-chō 366 |位置 = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-32|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}} |別名 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = Shinkō-sai |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Noki Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Noki Shrine''' (のきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Nogi-chō, {{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === Current deities are as follows. *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} In the entry for Noki station in Ou District of the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858}}'', Noki Ōkami appears. The original deity is considered to have been this Noki Ōkami, and there is a theory that Noki Ōkami and Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto are the same deity''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.40.. === History === It is identified with “Noki-sha,” a Shrine Bureau shrine in the Ou District section of ''Izumo no Kuni Fudoki,'' and with “Noki Shrine” in Ou District, Izumo Province, in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}''; but there is also a theory identifying it with “Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine” in Nogi District in the same text. Between this shrine and Kunitsu Daimyōjin in Kisa village (now {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shibusa Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}), there were disputes over candidate status twice: in the {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku}} era and early {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.42.. According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'', the {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} granted to Nogi deity was Junior Fifth Upper in {{ill|lt=867|Q23899|WD=Q23899}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635}} 9) and Senior Fifth Lower in {{ill|lt=871|Q23903|WD=Q23903}} (Jōgan 13). However, it is unclear whether these were granted to Noki Shrine or to Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine. When shrine buildings were lost in a fire in {{ill|lt=1611|Q6768|WD=Q6768}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 16), old records were also lost. In early modern times it was revered as the sōsha of Nogi District. It was ranked gōsha in {{ill|lt=Meiji 4|Q11512280|WD=Q11512280}}, then promoted to prefectural shrine in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}). === Ancient historic sites === In the precinct is the Noki Shrine site, considered to be dwelling remains from the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858}} to {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}}. Nearby are several {{ill|lt=round tumuli|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747}}, including Noki Shrine Okuin Kofun. === Festivals === *February - {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710}} *June 5 - {{ill|lt=Otaue Festival|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Rice-planting festival}} *June 30 - {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910}} *July 31 - {{ill|lt=Summer festival|Q11430090|WD=Q11430090}} *October 19 - {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}} *November 23 - {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518}} === Notes === === References === *{{ill|lt=Takine Yoshiyuki|Q17160660|WD=Q17160660}} ''Dictionary of Ancient Izumo'', 2001, {{ill|lt=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|WD=Q11501312}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Tanigawa Kenichi|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}, ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San’in'' (new edition), 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}} ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Noki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Noki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧県社]] [[Category:安来市の建築物]] [[Category:安来市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * のきじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1280005002351 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県安来市能義町366', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:能義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11612065}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Noki Shrine |画像 = [[File:Noki Shrine.jpg|270px]]
Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}{{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114}} Nogi-chō 366 |位置 = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-32|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}} |別名 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = Shinkō-sai |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Noki Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Noki Shrine''' (のきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Nogi-chō, {{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === Current deities are as follows. *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} In the entry for Noki station in Ou District of the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858}}'', Noki Ōkami appears. The original deity is considered to have been this Noki Ōkami, and there is a theory that Noki Ōkami and Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto are the same deity''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.40.. === History === It is identified with “Noki-sha,” a Shrine Bureau shrine in the Ou District section of ''Izumo no Kuni Fudoki,'' and with “Noki Shrine” in Ou District, Izumo Province, in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}''; but there is also a theory identifying it with “Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine” in Nogi District in the same text. Between this shrine and Kunitsu Daimyōjin in Kisa village (now {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shibusa Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}), there were disputes over candidate status twice: in the {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku}} era and early {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.42.. According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'', the {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} granted to Nogi deity was Junior Fifth Upper in {{ill|lt=867|Q23899|WD=Q23899}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635}} 9) and Senior Fifth Lower in {{ill|lt=871|Q23903|WD=Q23903}} (Jōgan 13). However, it is unclear whether these were granted to Noki Shrine or to Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine. When shrine buildings were lost in a fire in {{ill|lt=1611|Q6768|WD=Q6768}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 16), old records were also lost. In early modern times it was revered as the sōsha of Nogi District. It was ranked gōsha in {{ill|lt=Meiji 4|Q11512280|WD=Q11512280}}, then promoted to prefectural shrine in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}). === Ancient historic sites === In the precinct is the Noki Shrine site, considered to be dwelling remains from the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858}} to {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}}. Nearby are several {{ill|lt=round tumuli|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747}}, including Noki Shrine Okuin Kofun. === Festivals === *February - {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710}} *June 5 - {{ill|lt=Otaue Festival|UNKNOWN|lt=Rice-planting festival}} *June 30 - {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910}} *July 31 - {{ill|lt=Summer festival|Q11430090|WD=Q11430090}} *October 19 - {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}} *November 23 - {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518}} === Notes === === References === *{{ill|lt=Takine Yoshiyuki|Q17160660|WD=Q17160660}} ''Dictionary of Ancient Izumo'', 2001, {{ill|lt=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|WD=Q11501312}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Tanigawa Kenichi|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}, ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San’in'' (new edition), 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}} ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Noki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Noki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧県社]] [[Category:安来市の建築物]] [[Category:安来市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * のきじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1280005002351 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県安来市能義町366', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:能義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11612065}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Noki Shrine |画像 = [[File:Noki Shrine.jpg|270px]]
Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}{{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114}} Nogi-chō 366 |位置 = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-32|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}} |別名 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = Shinkō-sai |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Noki Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Noki Shrine''' (のきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Nogi-chō, {{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === Current deities are as follows. *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} In the entry for Noki station in Ou District of the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858}}'', Noki Ōkami appears. The original deity is considered to have been this Noki Ōkami, and there is a theory that Noki Ōkami and Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto are the same deity''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.40.. === History === It is identified with “Noki-sha,” a Shrine Bureau shrine in the Ou District section of ''Izumo no Kuni Fudoki,'' and with “Noki Shrine” in Ou District, Izumo Province, in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}''; but there is also a theory identifying it with “Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine” in Nogi District in the same text. Between this shrine and Kunitsu Daimyōjin in Kisa village (now {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shibusa Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}), there were disputes over candidate status twice: in the {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku}} era and early {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.42.. According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'', the {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} granted to Nogi deity was Junior Fifth Upper in {{ill|lt=867|Q23899|WD=Q23899}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635}} 9) and Senior Fifth Lower in {{ill|lt=871|Q23903|WD=Q23903}} (Jōgan 13). However, it is unclear whether these were granted to Noki Shrine or to Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine. When shrine buildings were lost in a fire in {{ill|lt=1611|Q6768|WD=Q6768}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 16), old records were also lost. In early modern times it was revered as the sōsha of Nogi District. It was ranked gōsha in {{ill|lt=Meiji 4|Q11512280|WD=Q11512280}}, then promoted to prefectural shrine in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}). === Ancient historic sites === In the precinct is the Noki Shrine site, considered to be dwelling remains from the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858}} to {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}}. Nearby are several {{ill|lt=round tumuli|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747}}, including Noki Shrine Okuin Kofun. === Festivals === *February - {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710}} *June 5 - {{ill|lt=Otaue Festival|UNKNOWN|lt=Rice-planting festival}} *June 30 - {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910}} *July 31 - {{ill|lt=Summer festival|Q11430090|WD=Q11430090}} *October 19 - {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}} *November 23 - {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518}} === Notes === === References === *{{ill|lt=Takine Yoshiyuki|Q17160660|WD=Q17160660}} ''Dictionary of Ancient Izumo'', 2001, {{ill|lt=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|WD=Q11501312}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Tanigawa Kenichi|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}, ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San’in'' (new edition), 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}} ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Noki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Noki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧県社]] [[Category:安来市の建築物]] [[Category:安来市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * のきじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1280005002351 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県安来市能義町366', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:能義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11612065}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Noki Shrine |画像 = [[File:Noki Shrine.jpg|270px]]
Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}{{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114}} Nogi-chō 366 |位置 = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-32|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}} |別名 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = Shinkō-sai |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Noki Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Noki Shrine''' (のきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Nogi-chō, {{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === Current deities are as follows. *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} In the entry for Noki station in Ou District of the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858}}'', Noki Ōkami appears. The original deity is considered to have been this Noki Ōkami, and there is a theory that Noki Ōkami and Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto are the same deity''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.40.. === History === It is identified with “Noki-sha,” a Shrine Bureau shrine in the Ou District section of ''Izumo no Kuni Fudoki,'' and with “Noki Shrine” in Ou District, Izumo Province, in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}''; but there is also a theory identifying it with “Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine” in Nogi District in the same text. Between this shrine and Kunitsu Daimyōjin in Kisa village (now {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shibusa Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}), there were disputes over candidate status twice: in the {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku}} era and early {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.42.. According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'', the {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} granted to Nogi deity was Junior Fifth Upper in {{ill|lt=867|Q23899|WD=Q23899}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635}} 9) and Senior Fifth Lower in {{ill|lt=871|Q23903|WD=Q23903}} (Jōgan 13). However, it is unclear whether these were granted to Noki Shrine or to Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine. When shrine buildings were lost in a fire in {{ill|lt=1611|Q6768|WD=Q6768}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 16), old records were also lost. In early modern times it was revered as the sōsha of Nogi District. It was ranked gōsha in {{ill|lt=Meiji 4|Q11512280|WD=Q11512280}}, then promoted to prefectural shrine in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}). === Ancient historic sites === In the precinct is the Noki Shrine site, considered to be dwelling remains from the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858}} to {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}}. Nearby are several {{ill|lt=round tumuli|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747}}, including Noki Shrine Okuin Kofun. === Festivals === *February - {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710}} *June 5 - {{ill|lt=Otaue Festival|UNKNOWN|lt=Rice-planting festival}} *June 30 - {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910}} *July 31 - {{ill|lt=Summer festival|Q11430090|WD=Q11430090}} *October 19 - {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}} *November 23 - {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518}} === Notes === === References === *{{ill|lt=Takine Yoshiyuki|Q17160660|WD=Q17160660}} ''Dictionary of Ancient Izumo'', 2001, {{ill|lt=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|WD=Q11501312}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Tanigawa Kenichi|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}, ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San’in'' (new edition), 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}} ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Noki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Noki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧県社]] [[Category:安来市の建築物]] [[Category:安来市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * のきじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1280005002351 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県安来市能義町366', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:能義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11612065}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Noki Shrine |画像 = [[File:Noki Shrine.jpg|270px]]
Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}{{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114}} Nogi-chō 366 |位置 = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-32|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}} |別名 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = Shinkō-sai |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Noki Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Noki Shrine''' (のきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Nogi-chō, {{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === Current deities are as follows. *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} In the entry for Noki station in Ou District of the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858}}'', Noki Ōkami appears. The original deity is considered to have been this Noki Ōkami, and there is a theory that Noki Ōkami and Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto are the same deity''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.40.. === History === It is identified with “Noki-sha,” a Shrine Bureau shrine in the Ou District section of ''Izumo no Kuni Fudoki,'' and with “Noki Shrine” in Ou District, Izumo Province, in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}''; but there is also a theory identifying it with “Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine” in Nogi District in the same text. Between this shrine and Kunitsu Daimyōjin in Kisa village (now {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shibusa Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}), there were disputes over candidate status twice: in the {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku}} era and early {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.42.. According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'', the {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} granted to Nogi deity was Junior Fifth Upper in {{ill|lt=867|Q23899|WD=Q23899}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635}} 9) and Senior Fifth Lower in {{ill|lt=871|Q23903|WD=Q23903}} (Jōgan 13). However, it is unclear whether these were granted to Noki Shrine or to Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine. When shrine buildings were lost in a fire in {{ill|lt=1611|Q6768|WD=Q6768}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 16), old records were also lost. In early modern times it was revered as the sōsha of Nogi District. It was ranked gōsha in {{ill|lt=Meiji 4|Q11512280|WD=Q11512280}}, then promoted to prefectural shrine in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}). === Ancient historic sites === In the precinct is the Noki Shrine site, considered to be dwelling remains from the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858}} to {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}}. Nearby are several {{ill|lt=round tumuli|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747}}, including Noki Shrine Okuin Kofun. === Festivals === *February - {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710}} *June 5 - {{ill|lt=Otaue Festival|UNKNOWN|lt=Rice-planting festival}} *June 30 - {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910}} *July 31 - {{ill|lt=Summer festival|Q11430090|WD=Q11430090}} *October 19 - {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}} *November 23 - {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518}} === Notes === === References === *{{ill|lt=Takine Yoshiyuki|Q17160660|WD=Q17160660}} ''Dictionary of Ancient Izumo'', 2001, {{ill|lt=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|WD=Q11501312}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Tanigawa Kenichi|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}, ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San’in'' (new edition), 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}} ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Noki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Noki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧県社]] [[Category:安来市の建築物]] [[Category:安来市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * のきじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1280005002351 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県安来市能義町366', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:能義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11612065}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Noki Shrine |画像 = [[File:Noki Shrine.jpg|270px]]
Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}{{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114}} Nogi-chō 366 |位置 = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-32|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}} |別名 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = Shinkō-sai |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Noki Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Noki Shrine''' (のきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Nogi-chō, {{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === Current deities are as follows. *{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} In the entry for Noki station in Ou District of the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858}}'', Noki Ōkami appears. The original deity is considered to have been this Noki Ōkami, and there is a theory that Noki Ōkami and Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto are the same deity''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.40.. === History === It is identified with “Noki-sha,” a Shrine Bureau shrine in the Ou District section of ''Izumo no Kuni Fudoki,'' and with “Noki Shrine” in Ou District, Izumo Province, in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}''; but there is also a theory identifying it with “Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine” in Nogi District in the same text. Between this shrine and Kunitsu Daimyōjin in Kisa village (now {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shibusa Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}), there were disputes over candidate status twice: in the {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku}} era and early {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.42.. According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'', the {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} granted to Nogi deity was Junior Fifth Upper in {{ill|lt=867|Q23899|WD=Q23899}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635}} 9) and Senior Fifth Lower in {{ill|lt=871|Q23903|WD=Q23903}} (Jōgan 13). However, it is unclear whether these were granted to Noki Shrine or to Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine. When shrine buildings were lost in a fire in {{ill|lt=1611|Q6768|WD=Q6768}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 16), old records were also lost. In early modern times it was revered as the sōsha of Nogi District. It was ranked gōsha in {{ill|lt=Meiji 4|Q11512280|WD=Q11512280}}, then promoted to prefectural shrine in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}). === Ancient historic sites === In the precinct is the Noki Shrine site, considered to be dwelling remains from the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858}} to {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}}. Nearby are several {{ill|lt=round tumuli|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747}}, including Noki Shrine Okuin Kofun. === Festivals === *February - {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710}} *June 5 - {{ill|lt=Otaue Festival|UNKNOWN|lt=Rice-planting festival}} *June 30 - {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910}} *July 31 - {{ill|lt=Summer festival|Q11430090|WD=Q11430090}} *October 19 - {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}} *November 23 - {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518}} === Notes === === References === *{{ill|lt=Takine Yoshiyuki|Q17160660|WD=Q17160660}} ''Dictionary of Ancient Izumo'', 2001, {{ill|lt=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|WD=Q11501312}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Tanigawa Kenichi|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}, ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San’in'' (new edition), 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}} ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Noki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Noki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧県社]] [[Category:安来市の建築物]] [[Category:安来市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * のきじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1280005002351 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県安来市能義町366', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:能義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11612065}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Noki Shrine |画像 = [[File:Noki Shrine.jpg|270px]]
Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}{{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114}} Nogi-chō 366 |位置 = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-32|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}} |別名 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = Shinkō-sai |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Noki Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Noki Shrine''' (のきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Nogi-chō, {{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === Current deities are as follows. *{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} In the entry for Noki station in Ou District of the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858}}'', Noki Ōkami appears. The original deity is considered to have been this Noki Ōkami, and there is a theory that Noki Ōkami and Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto are the same deity''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.40.. === History === It is identified with “Noki-sha,” a Shrine Bureau shrine in the Ou District section of ''Izumo no Kuni Fudoki,'' and with “Noki Shrine” in Ou District, Izumo Province, in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}''; but there is also a theory identifying it with “Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine” in Nogi District in the same text. Between this shrine and Kunitsu Daimyōjin in Kisa village (now {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shibusa Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}), there were disputes over candidate status twice: in the {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku}} era and early {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.42.. According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'', the {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} granted to Nogi deity was Junior Fifth Upper in {{ill|lt=867|Q23899|WD=Q23899}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635}} 9) and Senior Fifth Lower in {{ill|lt=871|Q23903|WD=Q23903}} (Jōgan 13). However, it is unclear whether these were granted to Noki Shrine or to Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine. When shrine buildings were lost in a fire in {{ill|lt=1611|Q6768|WD=Q6768}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 16), old records were also lost. In early modern times it was revered as the sōsha of Nogi District. It was ranked gōsha in {{ill|lt=Meiji 4|Q11512280|WD=Q11512280}}, then promoted to prefectural shrine in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}). === Ancient historic sites === In the precinct is the Noki Shrine site, considered to be dwelling remains from the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858}} to {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}}. Nearby are several {{ill|lt=round tumuli|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747}}, including Noki Shrine Okuin Kofun. === Festivals === *February - {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710}} *June 5 - {{ill|lt=Otaue Festival|UNKNOWN|lt=Rice-planting festival}} *June 30 - {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910}} *July 31 - {{ill|lt=Summer festival|Q11430090|WD=Q11430090}} *October 19 - {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}} *November 23 - {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518}} === Notes === === References === *{{ill|lt=Takine Yoshiyuki|Q17160660|WD=Q17160660}} ''Dictionary of Ancient Izumo'', 2001, {{ill|lt=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|WD=Q11501312}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Tanigawa Kenichi|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}, ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San’in'' (new edition), 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}} ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Noki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Noki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧県社]] [[Category:安来市の建築物]] [[Category:安来市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

link|Q11612065}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Noki Shrine |画像 = [[File:Noki Shrine.jpg|270px]]
Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}{{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114}} Nogi-chō 366 |位置 = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-32|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}} |別名 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = Shinkō-sai |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Noki Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Noki Shrine''' (のきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Nogi-chō, {{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === Current deities are as follows. *{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} In the entry for Noki station in Ou District of the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858}}'', Noki Ōkami appears. The original deity is considered to have been this Noki Ōkami, and there is a theory that Noki Ōkami and Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto are the same deity''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.40.. === History === It is identified with “Noki-sha,” a Shrine Bureau shrine in the Ou District section of ''Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki,'' and with “Noki Shrine” in Ou District, Izumo Province, in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}''; but there is also a theory identifying it with “Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine” in Nogi District in the same text. Between this shrine and Kunitsu Daimyōjin in Kisa village (now {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shibusa Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}), there were disputes over candidate status twice: in the {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku}} era and early {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.42.. According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'', the {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} granted to Nogi deity was Junior Fifth Upper in {{ill|lt=867|Q23899|WD=Q23899}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635}} 9) and Senior Fifth Lower in {{ill|lt=871|Q23903|WD=Q23903}} (Jōgan 13). However, it is unclear whether these were granted to Noki Shrine or to Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine. When shrine buildings were lost in a fire in {{ill|lt=1611|Q6768|WD=Q6768}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 16), old records were also lost. In early modern times it was revered as the sōsha of Nogi District. It was ranked gōsha in {{ill|lt=Meiji 4|Q11512280|WD=Q11512280}}, then promoted to prefectural shrine in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}). === Ancient historic sites === In the precinct is the Noki Shrine site, considered to be dwelling remains from the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858}} to {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}}. Nearby are several {{ill|lt=round tumuli|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747}}, including Noki Shrine Okuin Kofun. === Festivals === *February - {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710}} *June 5 - {{ill|lt=Otaue Festival|UNKNOWN|lt=Rice-planting festival}} *June 30 - {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910}} *July 31 - {{ill|lt=Summer festival|Q11430090|WD=Q11430090}} *October 19 - {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}} *November 23 - {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518}} === Notes === === References === *{{ill|lt=Takine Yoshiyuki|Q17160660|WD=Q17160660}} ''Dictionary of Ancient Izumo'', 2001, {{ill|lt=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|WD=Q11501312}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Tanigawa Kenichi|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}, ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San’in'' (new edition), 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}} ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Noki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Noki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧県社]] [[Category:安来市の建築物]] [[Category:安来市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * のきじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1280005002351 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県安来市能義町366', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:能義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11612065}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Noki Shrine |画像 = [[File:Noki Shrine.jpg|270px]]
Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114|1=Yasugi, Shimane}} Nogi-chō 366 |位置 = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-32|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = Shinkō-sai |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Noki Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Noki Shrine''' (のきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Nogi-chō, {{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114|1=Yasugi, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === Current deities are as follows. *{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} *{{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} In the entry for Noki station in Ou District of the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}}'', Noki Ōkami appears. The original deity is considered to have been this Noki Ōkami, and there is a theory that Noki Ōkami and Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto are the same deity''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.40.. === History === It is identified with “Noki-sha,” a Shrine Bureau shrine in the Ou District section of ''Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki,'' and with “Noki Shrine” in Ou District, Izumo Province, in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}''; but there is also a theory identifying it with “Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine” in Nogi District in the same text. Between this shrine and Kunitsu Daimyōjin in Kisa village (now {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shibusa Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}), there were disputes over candidate status twice: in the {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} era and early {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.42.. According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', the {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} granted to Nogi deity was Junior Fifth Upper in {{ill|lt=867|Q23899|WD=Q23899|1=Dizzy (series)}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} 9) and Senior Fifth Lower in {{ill|lt=871|Q23903|WD=Q23903|1=Tworki, Siedlce County}} (Jōgan 13). However, it is unclear whether these were granted to Noki Shrine or to Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine. When shrine buildings were lost in a fire in {{ill|lt=1611|Q6768|WD=Q6768|1=1611}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 16), old records were also lost. In early modern times it was revered as the sōsha of Nogi District. It was ranked gōsha in {{ill|lt=Meiji 4|Q11512280|WD=Q11512280|1=Meiji – year 4}}, then promoted to prefectural shrine in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}). === Ancient historic sites === In the precinct is the Noki Shrine site, considered to be dwelling remains from the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858|1=Yayoi period}} to {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}}. Nearby are several {{ill|lt=round tumuli|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}}, including Noki Shrine Okuin Kofun. === Festivals === *February - {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} *June 5 - {{ill|lt=Otaue Festival|UNKNOWN|lt=Rice-planting festival}} *June 30 - {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910|1=Ōharae-shiki}} *July 31 - {{ill|lt=Summer festival|Q11430090|WD=Q11430090|1=Summer Matsuri}} *October 19 - {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} *November 23 - {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} === Notes === === References === *{{ill|lt=Takine Yoshiyuki|Q17160660|WD=Q17160660|1=Yoshiyuki Takioto}} ''Dictionary of Ancient Izumo'', 2001, {{ill|lt=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|WD=Q11501312|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Tanigawa Kenichi|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}, ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San’in'' (new edition), 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}} ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Noki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Noki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧県社]] [[Category:安来市の建築物]] [[Category:安来市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * のきじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1280005002351 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県安来市能義町366', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:能義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11612065}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Noki Shrine |画像 = [[File:Noki Shrine.jpg|270px]]
Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114|1=Yasugi, Shimane}} Nogi-chō 366 |位置 = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-32|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = Shinkō-sai |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Noki Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Noki Shrine''' (のきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Nogi-chō, {{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114|1=Yasugi, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === Current deities are as follows. *{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} *{{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} In the entry for Noki station in Ou District of the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}}'', Noki Ōkami appears. The original deity is considered to have been this Noki Ōkami, and there is a theory that Noki Ōkami and Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto are the same deity''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.40.. === History === It is identified with “Noki-sha,” a Shrine Bureau shrine in the Ou District section of ''Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki,'' and with “Noki Shrine” in Ou District, Izumo Province, in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}''; but there is also a theory identifying it with “Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine” in Nogi District in the same text. Between this shrine and Kunitsu Daimyōjin in Kisa village (now {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shibusa Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}), there were disputes over candidate status twice: in the {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} era and early {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.42.. According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', the {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} granted to Nogi deity was Junior Fifth Upper in {{ill|lt=867|Q23899|WD=Q23899|1=Dizzy (series)}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} 9) and Senior Fifth Lower in {{ill|lt=871|Q23903|WD=Q23903|1=Tworki, Siedlce County}} (Jōgan 13). However, it is unclear whether these were granted to Noki Shrine or to Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine. When shrine buildings were lost in a fire in {{ill|lt=1611|Q6768|WD=Q6768|1=1611}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 16), old records were also lost. In early modern times it was revered as the sōsha of Nogi District. It was ranked gōsha in {{ill|lt=Meiji 4|Q11512280|WD=Q11512280|1=Meiji – year 4}}, then promoted to prefectural shrine in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}). === Ancient historic sites === In the precinct is the Noki Shrine site, considered to be dwelling remains from the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858|1=Yayoi period}} to {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}}. Nearby are several {{ill|lt=round tumuli|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}}, including Noki Shrine Okuin Kofun. === Festivals === *February - {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} *June 5 - {{ill|lt=Otaue Festival|UNKNOWN|lt=Rice-planting festival}} *June 30 - {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910|1=Ōharae-shiki}} *July 31 - {{ill|lt=Summer festival|Q11430090|WD=Q11430090|1=Summer Matsuri}} *October 19 - {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} *November 23 - {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} === Notes === === References === *{{ill|lt=Takine Yoshiyuki|Q17160660|WD=Q17160660|1=Yoshiyuki Takioto}} ''Dictionary of Ancient Izumo'', 2001, {{ill|lt=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|WD=Q11501312|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Tanigawa Kenichi|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}, ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San’in'' (new edition), 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}} ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Noki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Noki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧県社]] [[Category:安来市の建築物]] [[Category:安来市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * のきじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1280005002351 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県安来市能義町366', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:能義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11612065}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Noki Shrine |画像 = [[File:Noki Shrine.jpg|270px]]
Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114|1=Yasugi, Shimane}} Nogi-chō 366 |位置 = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-32|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = Shinkō-sai |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Noki Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Noki Shrine''' (のきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Nogi-chō, {{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114|1=Yasugi, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === Current deities are as follows. *{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} *{{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} In the entry for Noki station in Ou District of the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}}'', Noki Ōkami appears. The original deity is considered to have been this Noki Ōkami, and there is a theory that Noki Ōkami and Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto are the same deity''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.40.. === History === It is identified with “Noki-sha,” a Shrine Bureau shrine in the Ou District section of ''Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki,'' and with “Noki Shrine” in Ou District, Izumo Province, in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}''; but there is also a theory identifying it with “Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine” in Nogi District in the same text. Between this shrine and Kunitsu Daimyōjin in Kisa village (now {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shibusa Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}), there were disputes over candidate status twice: in the {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} era and early {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.42.. According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', the {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} granted to Nogi deity was Junior Fifth Upper in {{ill|lt=867|Q23899|WD=Q23899|1=Dizzy (series)}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} 9) and Senior Fifth Lower in {{ill|lt=871|Q23903|WD=Q23903|1=Tworki, Siedlce County}} (Jōgan 13). However, it is unclear whether these were granted to Noki Shrine or to Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine. When shrine buildings were lost in a fire in {{ill|lt=1611|Q6768|WD=Q6768|1=1611}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 16), old records were also lost. In early modern times it was revered as the sōsha of Nogi District. It was ranked gōsha in {{ill|lt=Meiji 4|Q11512280|WD=Q11512280|1=Meiji – year 4}}, then promoted to prefectural shrine in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}). === Ancient historic sites === In the precinct is the Noki Shrine site, considered to be dwelling remains from the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858|1=Yayoi period}} to {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}}. Nearby are several {{ill|lt=round tumuli|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}}, including Noki Shrine Okuin Kofun. === Festivals === *February - {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} *June 5 - {{ill|lt=Otaue Festival|UNKNOWN|lt=Rice-planting festival}} *June 30 - {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910|1=Ōharae-shiki}} *July 31 - {{ill|lt=Summer festival|Q11430090|WD=Q11430090|1=Summer Matsuri}} *October 19 - {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} *November 23 - {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} === Notes === === References === *{{ill|lt=Takine Yoshiyuki|Q17160660|WD=Q17160660|1=Yoshiyuki Takioto}} ''Dictionary of Ancient Izumo'', 2001, {{ill|lt=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|WD=Q11501312|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Tanigawa Kenichi|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}, ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San’in'' (new edition), 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}} ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Noki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Noki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧県社]] [[Category:安来市の建築物]] [[Category:安来市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * のきじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1280005002351 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県安来市能義町366', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:能義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11612065}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Noki Shrine |画像 = [[File:Noki Shrine.jpg|270px]]
Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114|1=Yasugi, Shimane}} Nogi-chō 366 |位置 = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-32|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = Shinkō-sai |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Noki Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Noki Shrine''' (のきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Nogi-chō, {{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114|1=Yasugi, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === Current deities are as follows. *{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} *{{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} In the entry for Noki station in Ou District of the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}}'', Noki Ōkami appears. The original deity is considered to have been this Noki Ōkami, and there is a theory that Noki Ōkami and Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto are the same deity''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.40.. === History === It is identified with “Noki-sha,” a Shrine Bureau shrine in the Ou District section of ''Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki,'' and with “Noki Shrine” in Ou District, Izumo Province, in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}''; but there is also a theory identifying it with “Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine” in Nogi District in the same text. Between this shrine and Kunitsu Daimyōjin in Kisa village (now {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shibusa Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}), there were disputes over candidate status twice: in the {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} era and early {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.42.. According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', the {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} granted to Nogi deity was Junior Fifth Upper in {{ill|lt=867|Q23899|WD=Q23899|1=Dizzy (series)}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} 9) and Senior Fifth Lower in {{ill|lt=871|Q23903|WD=Q23903|1=Tworki, Siedlce County}} (Jōgan 13). However, it is unclear whether these were granted to Noki Shrine or to Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine. When shrine buildings were lost in a fire in {{ill|lt=1611|Q6768|WD=Q6768|1=1611}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 16), old records were also lost. In early modern times it was revered as the sōsha of Nogi District. It was ranked gōsha in {{ill|lt=Meiji 4|Q11512280|WD=Q11512280|1=Meiji – year 4}}, then promoted to prefectural shrine in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}). === Ancient historic sites === In the precinct is the Noki Shrine site, considered to be dwelling remains from the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858|1=Yayoi period}} to {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}}. Nearby are several {{ill|lt=round tumuli|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}}, including Noki Shrine Okuin Kofun. === Festivals === *February - {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} *June 5 - {{ill|lt=Otaue Festival|UNKNOWN|lt=Rice-planting festival}} *June 30 - {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910|1=Ōharae-shiki}} *July 31 - {{ill|lt=Summer festival|Q11430090|WD=Q11430090|1=Summer Matsuri}} *October 19 - {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} *November 23 - {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} === Notes === === References === *{{ill|lt=Takine Yoshiyuki|Q17160660|WD=Q17160660|1=Yoshiyuki Takioto}} ''Dictionary of Ancient Izumo'', 2001, {{ill|lt=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|WD=Q11501312|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Tanigawa Kenichi|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}, ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San’in'' (new edition), 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}} ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Noki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Noki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧県社]] [[Category:安来市の建築物]] [[Category:安来市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * のきじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1280005002351 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県安来市能義町366', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:能義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11612065}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Noki Shrine |画像 = [[File:Noki Shrine.jpg|270px]]
Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114|1=Yasugi, Shimane}} Nogi-chō 366 |位置 = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-32|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = Shinkō-sai |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Noki Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Noki Shrine''' (のきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Nogi-chō, {{ill|lt=Yasugi|Q851114|WD=Q851114|1=Yasugi, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === Current deities are as follows. *{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} *{{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} In the entry for Noki station in Ou District of the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}}'', Noki Ōkami appears. The original deity is considered to have been this Noki Ōkami, and there is a theory that Noki Ōkami and Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto are the same deity''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.40.. === History === It is identified with “Noki-sha,” a Shrine Bureau shrine in the Ou District section of ''Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki,'' and with “Noki Shrine” in Ou District, Izumo Province, in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}''; but there is also a theory identifying it with “Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine” in Nogi District in the same text. Between this shrine and Kunitsu Daimyōjin in Kisa village (now {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shibusa Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}), there were disputes over candidate status twice: in the {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} era and early {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}''Gods of Japan (7)'', p.42.. According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', the {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} granted to Nogi deity was Junior Fifth Upper in {{ill|lt=867|Q23899|WD=Q23899|1=Dizzy (series)}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} 9) and Senior Fifth Lower in {{ill|lt=871|Q23903|WD=Q23903|1=Tworki, Siedlce County}} (Jōgan 13). However, it is unclear whether these were granted to Noki Shrine or to Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto Shrine. When shrine buildings were lost in a fire in {{ill|lt=1611|Q6768|WD=Q6768|1=1611}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 16), old records were also lost. In early modern times it was revered as the sōsha of Nogi District. It was ranked gōsha in {{ill|lt=Meiji 4|Q11512280|WD=Q11512280|1=Meiji – year 4}}, then promoted to prefectural shrine in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}). === Ancient historic sites === In the precinct is the Noki Shrine site, considered to be dwelling remains from the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858|1=Yayoi period}} to {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}}. Nearby are several {{ill|lt=round tumuli|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}}, including Noki Shrine Okuin Kofun. === Festivals === *February - {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} *June 5 - {{ill|lt=Otaue Festival|UNKNOWN|lt=Rice-planting festival}} *June 30 - {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910|1=Ōharae-shiki}} *July 31 - {{ill|lt=Summer festival|Q11430090|WD=Q11430090|1=Summer Matsuri}} *October 19 - {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} *November 23 - {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} === Notes === === References === *{{ill|lt=Takine Yoshiyuki|Q17160660|WD=Q17160660|1=Yoshiyuki Takioto}} ''Dictionary of Ancient Izumo'', 2001, {{ill|lt=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|WD=Q11501312|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Tanigawa Kenichi|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}, ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San’in'' (new edition), 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}} ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Noki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Noki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の旧県社]] [[Category:安来市の建築物]] [[Category:安来市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]
